Title: Product Detail - Basic Info
Preconditions: Product exists in catalog
Assertions:
[DOM] Product name displayed and non-empty (in H1 or title element)
[DOM] Price displayed and > 0 (matches /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/ — not zero, not placeholder)
[DOM] Product description visible
[DOM] At least one product image loaded (non-empty src)
[DOM] SKU value shown (non-empty)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET product detail page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: PDP returns 4xx/5xx; product name missing; price shows 0 or placeholder; images fail to load; SKU absent; console TypeError

Title: Product Detail - Image Gallery
Preconditions: Product with multiple images exists in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a product detail page with multiple images on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify thumbnail strip is visible
[ACT] Click a thumbnail image
[WAIT] Wait for main image to change
[ASSERT] Verify main image updates to match clicked thumbnail
[ACT] Click zoom or enlarge button on main image
[WAIT] Wait for zoom/modal to open
[ASSERT] Verify zoom functionality works
Assertions:
[DOM] Thumbnail strip visible with multiple images
[STATE] Clicking thumbnail updates the main image
[DOM] Zoom/enlarge control present
[STATE] Zoom or modal opens on click
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] image requests return HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Thumbnails not clickable; main image not updating; zoom control absent; images return 4xx; console TypeError

Title: Product Variations - B2C Style
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Product with size and color variations exists in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a configurable product page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify variation selectors (color, size) are visible
[ACT] Select a color option
[WAIT] Wait for price/availability to update
[ACT] Select a size option
[WAIT] Wait for selection to register
[ASSERT] Verify price reflects selected combination (price > 0)
[ACT] Click 'Add to cart'
[WAIT] Wait for cart confirmation
Assertions:
[DOM] Color and size selectors rendered
[DOM] Price updates to reflect selected variant (price > 0, matches /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/)
[STATE] Correct SKU variant added to cart (not base product)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on variant selection or add-to-cart
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Variation selectors missing; price not updating on selection; wrong variant in cart; add-to-cart fails; console TypeError
Cleanup: Remove added item from cart after test

Title: Product - Related Products
Preconditions: Product with related items configured exists in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[SCROLL] Scroll down to related products section
[ASSERT] Verify related products section is visible
[ACT] Click on a related product
[WAIT] Wait for related product PDP to load
[ASSERT] Verify navigation to related product
Assertions:
[DOM] Related products section visible below main product info
[DOM] Related product cards show image and non-empty name
[NAV] Clicking a related product navigates to its PDP
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET related product requests return HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Related products section absent; section empty when products exist; navigation to related product fails; console TypeError

Title: Category Selector Badge - Size xs Renders Correctly
Preconditions: Category selector component visible in category navigation
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a top-level category page that shows subcategory navigation on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Locate the category selector component
[ASSERT] Observe count badges on category items
[ASSERT] Verify badges render consistently across multiple category items
Assertions:
[DOM] Category item count badges render at xs size
[DOM] Badge count is readable (not clipped)
[DOM] No layout overflow caused by smaller badge size
[DOM] Badge appearance consistent across different category items
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Badges overflowing category item containers; badge count not readable; inconsistent badge size across items; console TypeError

Title: Add to Compare from Catalog Card
Preconditions: Category page with product cards; at least 2 comparable products visible. Compare uses browser localStorage so it works for guests and authenticated users alike (no sign-in required).
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page with product cards on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify each product card shows a button labeled 'Add to Compare' (in the card action row, next to 'Add to list')
[ACT] Note the current count on the header 'Compare' nav link (badge absent or 0 when list empty)
[ACT] Click 'Add to Compare' on the first product card
[WAIT] Wait for the success toast and header badge to update
[ASSERT] Verify the card's button toggled to 'Remove from Compare' (aria-pressed=true)
[ASSERT] Verify the header 'Compare' link badge count incremented by 1
Assertions:
[DOM] Per-card control is a button labeled 'Add to Compare' that toggles to 'Remove from Compare'; aria-pressed reflects compare state
[DOM] The compare count is shown ONLY as a numeric badge inside the header 'Compare' nav link (href=/compare) — it increments to 1 after adding
[STATE] No floating/sticky compare bar exists anywhere on the page (count lives in the header link); a transient success toast appears after add
[STATE] Compare selection persists in browser localStorage
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on add-to-compare (localStorage-backed; may be no network call)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: 'Add to Compare' button missing on cards; clicking does not toggle the button to 'Remove from Compare'; header Compare badge does not increment; console TypeError
Cleanup: Open /compare and click 'Clear list' (or remove the added item)

Title: Compare Page Display
Preconditions: 2 or more products added to the compare bar
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Add 2 or more products to compare bar
[ACT] Click 'Compare' in compare bar or navigate to /compare
[WAIT] Wait for compare page to load
[ASSERT] Verify products displayed side by side
[ASSERT] Verify spec/attribute rows are shown for comparison
Assertions:
[NAV] Compare page loads at /compare or similar URL
[DOM] Products displayed in side-by-side column layout
[DOM] Product name, image, and price shown at top of each column (price matches /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/)
[DOM] Specification attribute rows listed for comparison
[DOM] Each product has an 'Add to Cart' button
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET /compare returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Compare page returns 4xx; products not in side-by-side layout; attributes missing; Add to Cart absent; console TypeError

Title: Compare Limit
Preconditions: Category page with compare active; currently at maximum allowed products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Add products to compare until the maximum limit is reached
[ASSERT] Verify compare bar shows maximum count
[ACT] Attempt to add one more product beyond the limit
[WAIT] Wait for limit message
[ASSERT] Verify limit message is displayed
[ASSERT] Verify extra product is not added to compare bar
Assertions:
[DOM] System shows limit message (e.g., 'You can compare up to X products')
[STATE] Over-limit product not added to compare bar
[MATH] Compare bar still shows only the allowed maximum count (unchanged after over-limit attempt)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 5xx on over-limit attempt
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: No limit message shown; over-limit product added silently; compare bar count exceeds maximum; console TypeError

Title: Remove from Compare
Preconditions: 2 or more products added to compare; on /compare page
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the compare page at {{FRONT_URL}}/compare
[ASSERT] Verify remove button (X or Remove) visible per product column
[ACT] Click remove on one product
[WAIT] Wait for that product column to disappear
[ASSERT] Verify remaining products still displayed
[ASSERT] Verify compare bar count decrements
Assertions:
[DOM] Remove/close button available per product column on compare page
[STATE] Removed product column disappears from comparison
[DOM] Remaining products still shown correctly
[MATH] Compare bar count decrements by 1 after removal
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on remove request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Remove button not visible; removing one product removes all; remaining products layout broken; compare count not updating; console TypeError

Title: Compare Empty State
Preconditions: No products in compare (fresh session or after clearing)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/compare with no products in compare bar
[ASSERT] Verify empty state UI is shown
Assertions:
[DOM] Compare page loads without JavaScript error
[DOM] Empty state message shown (e.g., 'No products to compare')
[DOM] Call-to-action present ('Browse catalog' or 'Add products to compare' link)
[NAV] CTA link navigates back to catalog
[DOM] No broken layout
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET /compare returns HTTP 200 (not 4xx)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Compare page returns 4xx on empty state; no empty state message; CTA link broken; layout broken without products; console TypeError

Title: B2B Virtual Catalog Navigation - Org User Sees Assigned Products
Preconditions: Org user logged in; virtual catalog assigned to org with subset of products; physical catalog has additional products not in virtual catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Log in as org user at {{FRONT_URL}} using {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}} / {{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
[NAV] Navigate to main catalog page
[WAIT] Wait for product listing to load
[ASSERT] Verify only products assigned to the org's virtual catalog are displayed
[ACT] Search for a product known to exist in the physical catalog but NOT in the virtual catalog (confirm exclusion at runtime by checking xAPI with both storeId and without)
[WAIT] Wait for search results
[ASSERT] Verify the excluded product does not appear in results
[ACT] Search for a product known to exist in the virtual catalog (resolve at runtime via xAPI products query scoped to the org's storeId)
[WAIT] Wait for search results
[ASSERT] Verify the included product appears in results
Assertions:
[DOM] Product listing shows only products from the virtual catalog assigned to the org
[STATE] Products outside the virtual catalog are not visible in search or browse
[DOM] Product cards display prices in the org-specific currency (price > 0)
[MATH] Product count > 0 (org has at least one product in virtual catalog)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 with correct catalog scope
[API] Verify storeId and catalogId in GraphQL request match org's virtual catalog
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Products from physical catalog leaking into virtual catalog view; org user seeing full catalog instead of virtual subset; search returning products outside virtual catalog scope; console TypeError

Title: Virtual Catalog Inheritance - Physical Catalog Products Appear in Virtual View
Preconditions: Admin has configured a virtual catalog that inherits products from a physical catalog; org user has access to the virtual catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Log in as org user at {{FRONT_URL}}
[NAV] Navigate to a category that exists in the virtual catalog (inherited from physical)
[WAIT] Wait for category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify products from the physical catalog appear under the virtual catalog category (count > 0)
[ASSERT] Verify product details (name, price, image) are populated (non-empty values)
[ACT] Click on an inherited product to open its PDP
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load
[ASSERT] Verify PDP loads with correct product information (non-empty name, price > 0)
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumbs reflect the virtual catalog category hierarchy (not physical)
Assertions:
[DOM] Inherited products display in the virtual catalog category listing (count > 0)
[DOM] Product name, price, and image are non-empty and populated
[NAV] PDP loads successfully for inherited products
[DOM] Breadcrumbs show virtual catalog path, not physical catalog path
[STATE] URL reflects virtual catalog category structure
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET product PDP returns HTTP 200
[API] GraphQL product query returns catalogId matching virtual catalog
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Inherited products missing from virtual catalog; product details missing or empty; breadcrumbs showing physical catalog path; PDP returning 4xx; console TypeError

Title: PDP Description & Properties Inline Sections
Preconditions: Product exists with description content and specification properties defined
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}} that has a description and properties
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify the PDP renders sections as INLINE, always-open stacked blocks — there is NO tab navigation (no tab/tablist/tabpanel roles) and NO accordion expand/collapse
[ASSERT] Verify a 'Description' section (heading level 2) is present with non-empty product description text
[COND: description is long] [ACT] Click the 'See more' link inside the Description block
[COND: description is long] [ASSERT] Verify 'See more' expands the truncated description text in place (it is a text-truncation toggle, not a section switcher)
[ASSERT] Verify a 'Properties' section (heading level 2) is present rendering property name/value rows
Assertions:
[DOM] PDP shows inline always-open blocks; NO tab/tablist/tabpanel roles and NO accordion exist
[DOM] 'Description' h2 block visible with non-empty description content (and optional 'See more' truncation toggle)
[DOM] 'Properties' h2 block visible with property label/value rows
[STATE] All present sections are visible simultaneously on a single scrollable page (no tab switching)
[STATE] A Reviews/Ratings section is NOT expected (not implemented on the storefront PDP) — do not assert it
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
[CONSOLE] no Vue hydration warnings on tab switch
Failure_Signals: Tabs not rendering; description content empty; specifications not loading; reviews tab crashing; multiple tab contents visible simultaneously; console TypeError

Title: In-Stock Product PDP - Availability Badge and Add to Cart Enabled
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
An in-stock product with inventory > 0 exists in the B2B virtual catalog; user authenticated or guest session active
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any product detail page for an in-stock product on {{FRONT_URL}} (resolve at runtime: xAPI products query with availabilityData.isInStock=true)
[WAIT] Wait for the PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify an availability indicator (e.g., 'In stock' or green badge) is displayed near the product price or title
[ASSERT] Verify the quantity stepper (+) is visible and enabled (not grayed out or disabled)
[ACT] Click the quantity stepper (+) to add the product to cart
[WAIT] Wait for cart confirmation (mini-cart or toast or badge increment)
[ASSERT] Verify the cart badge count increments by 1
Assertions:
[DOM] Availability indicator visible and shows in-stock status
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) is enabled (not disabled)
[MATH] Cart badge increments by 1 after using the quantity stepper (+)
[DOM] No 'Out of stock' or 'Unavailable' label visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST add-to-cart returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Availability indicator absent; quantity stepper (+) disabled for in-stock product; cart badge not incrementing; add-to-cart returning 4xx; console TypeError
Cleanup: Remove added product from cart after test

Title: Out-of-Stock Product PDP - Unavailable Indicator and Add to Cart Disabled
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
An out-of-stock product (inventory = 0 or availability = false) exists in the B2B virtual catalog; fixture: {{OOS_SKU}} (see test-data/products/test-products.csv PROD-101)
Steps:
[NAV] Resolve the out-of-stock product PDP URL at runtime using {{OOS_SKU}} — search for the SKU or navigate directly; if {{OOS_SKU}} is not provisioned, skip this case (SEED REQUIRED: OOS product fixture)
[WAIT] Wait for the PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify an out-of-stock indicator is displayed (e.g., 'Out of stock' or 'Unavailable')
[ASSERT] Verify the quantity stepper (+) is absent or disabled, or replaced with an alternative CTA
[ASSERT] Verify the product price is still displayed even if unavailable
[ACT] Attempt to use the quantity stepper (+) if visible but disabled
[ASSERT] Verify no item is added to the cart (cart badge unchanged)
Assertions:
[DOM] Out-of-stock indicator clearly visible near product title or price
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) is disabled or absent for out-of-stock product
[MATH] Cart badge count does not change after attempting add-to-cart on disabled button
[DOM] Product price still displayed (not hidden)
[STATE] No way to add the out-of-stock product to cart through the PDP
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no add-to-cart POST fired when button is disabled
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Out-of-stock indicator absent; quantity stepper (+) enabled for out-of-stock product; cart badge incrementing for out-of-stock product; console TypeError

Title: Currency Switch on Category Page Updates All Product Prices
Preconditions: Multiple currencies configured; category has products with prices in at least 2 currencies; currency switcher visible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category listing page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for product cards to load
[ASSERT] Note the current currency symbol and record prices of the first 3 visible product cards
[ACT] Locate the currency switcher (header or footer) and select any alternative currency different from the current one
[WAIT] Wait for the page to reload or re-render with updated prices
[ASSERT] Verify the currency symbol has changed to the selected currency
[ASSERT] Verify the prices on all visible product cards have updated (each price matches /^[A-Z€£¥]{1,3}[0-9,\s]+\.[0-9]{2}$/ pattern for the new currency)
[ASSERT] Verify the updated prices reflect currency-specific price list values (not exchange-rate converted values)
Assertions:
[DOM] Currency symbol in product cards changes to the selected currency after switch
[STATE] Prices are updated to currency-specific price list values (not exchange-rate conversions)
[MATH] At least 3 product cards show prices in the new currency symbol format
[DOM] Currency switcher reflects the currently selected currency
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] graphql request includes new currency code after switch
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on currency switch
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Currency symbol unchanged after switch; prices not updating after currency change; prices showing exchange-rate conversion instead of price-list values; console TypeError

Title: Currency Switch on PDP Updates Product Price to Price List Value
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Multiple currencies configured; product has prices defined in at least 2 currency price lists; currency switcher visible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully render
[ASSERT] Note the current currency symbol and displayed price
[ACT] Locate the currency switcher and select any alternative currency different from the current one
[WAIT] Wait for the PDP to update
[ASSERT] Verify the price now shows in the selected currency (price > 0, matches currency symbol pattern)
[ASSERT] Verify the currency symbol in the price matches the selected currency
[ASSERT] Verify the quantity stepper (+) is still functional after currency switch
Assertions:
[DOM] Price on PDP updates to a currency-specific price list value after switch (price > 0)
[STATE] The price reflects the exact value from the alternative currency price list (not a calculated exchange rate)
[DOM] Currency symbol adjacent to price matches the selected currency
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) remains enabled and functional after currency change
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (product) includes new currencyCode in variables after switch
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: PDP price not updating after currency switch; price showing exchange-rate conversion; currency symbol not matching selected currency; quantity stepper broken after switch; console TypeError

Title: Product With No Price in Selected Currency Shows Correct Unavailability State
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
A product exists that has a price in the default currency but no price defined in at least one other available currency; currency switcher visible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to the PDP of a product that has a price in the default currency but no price in an alternative currency (verify at runtime: query product prices for multiple currencies; find a currency with no price entry)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load in default currency
[ASSERT] Verify product is available with a price > 0 and quantity stepper (+) enabled
[ACT] Switch currency to the alternative currency that has no price for this product
[WAIT] Wait for the page to update
[ASSERT] Verify the product either shows a 'Not available in this currency' state OR hides the price and disables the quantity stepper (+)
[ASSERT] Verify no zero-value placeholder price (e.g., $0.00) is shown for the unavailable currency
Assertions:
[STATE] Product not priced in the selected currency is clearly marked as unavailable or price-hidden
[DOM] No zero-value placeholder price displayed for a product with no price in selected currency
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) is disabled or absent when no price is defined for the currency
[DOM] Meaningful message or indicator communicates the product is not available in the selected currency
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no add-to-cart POST for zero-price product
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Product showing zero price for a currency with no price list entry; quantity stepper (+) enabled for zero-price product; no indication to user that product is unavailable in selected currency; console TypeError

Title: Recently Viewed Products Section Appears on PDP After Browsing Multiple Products
Preconditions: User visits at least 2 different product PDPs in the same session; recently viewed feature enabled in storefront config
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[NAV] Navigate to any first product detail page (Product A)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load
[SCROLL] Scroll down to check if 'Recently Viewed' section exists (may be empty for first product)
[NAV] Navigate to a second different product detail page (Product B)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load
[SCROLL] Scroll down the PDP to find the 'Recently Viewed' section
[ASSERT] Verify 'Recently Viewed' or equivalent section is now visible
[ASSERT] Verify Product A appears in the recently viewed section
[ACT] Click on Product A in the recently viewed section
[WAIT] Wait for PDP of Product A to load
[ASSERT] Verify navigation to Product A PDP was successful
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Recently Viewed' section appears on the PDP after visiting at least 2 products
[DOM] Previously visited product listed in the recently viewed section with non-empty name and image
[NAV] Clicking a recently viewed item navigates to that product PDP
[STATE] Currently viewed product is NOT listed in its own recently viewed section
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on recently viewed data request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Recently viewed section absent after visiting multiple products; visited products not appearing in section; clicking recently viewed item fails to navigate; current product appearing in its own recently viewed list; console TypeError

Title: Out-of-Stock PDP Display - Clear Unavailability Messaging
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
An out-of-stock product with inventory = 0 exists and is accessible via PDP URL; fixture: {{OOS_SKU}} (see test-data/products/test-products.csv PROD-101)
Steps:
[NAV] Resolve the out-of-stock product PDP URL at runtime using {{OOS_SKU}}; if {{OOS_SKU}} is not provisioned, skip this case (SEED REQUIRED: OOS product fixture)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify an out-of-stock or unavailability message is prominently displayed (e.g., 'Out of stock' or 'Currently unavailable' or 'Sold out')
[ASSERT] Verify the quantity stepper (+) is disabled or replaced with an alternative CTA
[ASSERT] Verify the product name image description and specifications are still visible
[ASSERT] Verify no quantity input field allows selecting quantities or that the input is disabled
[SCROLL] Scroll through the full PDP to check for any UI elements that incorrectly suggest the product is purchasable
Assertions:
[DOM] Clear out-of-stock label displayed near the product title or price area or main CTA zone
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) disabled or replaced with non-purchase CTA
[DOM] Product name image and description fully visible despite out-of-stock status
[STATE] Quantity selector disabled or absent for out-of-stock product
[DOM] No misleading 'In stock' or 'Available' label present
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no add-to-cart POST request fired for out-of-stock product
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Out-of-stock message absent; quantity stepper (+) enabled for zero-inventory product; product information hidden; quantity input active for out-of-stock product; console TypeError

Title: Low Stock Indicator Displayed When Product Quantity Is Near Threshold
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
A product with low inventory (at or below the configured low-stock warning threshold) exists; low stock warning configured in store settings; fixture: {{LOW_STOCK_SKU}} (see test-data/products/test-products.csv PROD-102)
Steps:
[NAV] Resolve the low-stock product PDP URL at runtime using {{LOW_STOCK_SKU}}; if {{LOW_STOCK_SKU}} is not provisioned, skip this case (SEED REQUIRED: low-stock product fixture)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify a low stock warning message is displayed (e.g., 'Only X left!' or 'Low stock' or 'Limited availability')
[ASSERT] Verify the quantity stepper (+) is enabled (product still purchasable)
[ASSERT] Verify the low stock message shows the remaining quantity or a qualitative indicator
[ASSERT] Verify the warning message is visually distinct (e.g., amber color or warning icon)
Assertions:
[DOM] Low stock warning message visible on PDP when inventory is at or below configured threshold
[STATE] Quantity stepper (+) remains enabled when product is low stock but not zero
[DOM] Warning message communicates urgency (quantity count or low stock label)
[DOM] Warning message is visually distinct from standard product information
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] product availability data returned in GraphQL response includes low-stock signal
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Low stock warning absent when inventory below threshold; quantity stepper (+) incorrectly disabled for low-stock non-zero product; warning message not visually distinct; console TypeError

Title: Add Product to Compare from PDP
Preconditions: Multiple products exist in catalog. Compare is localStorage-backed and works for guests and authenticated users.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] PDP fully loaded with product details
[ASSERT] Verify the PDP action row (sidebar, alongside 'Add to list' / Share / Print) contains a button 'Add to Compare'
[ACT] Click 'Add to Compare' on the PDP
[WAIT] Wait for the success toast and header badge to update
Assertions:
[DOM] PDP 'Add to Compare' button toggles to 'Remove from Compare' with aria-pressed=true
[STATE] Product added to compare list (button reflects selected state; transient success toast shown)
[DOM] The header 'Compare' nav link (href=/compare) badge shows 1 — there is NO floating/sticky compare widget at the bottom of the page
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors during compare operation (localStorage-backed)
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: 'Add to Compare' button missing or disabled on PDP; button does not toggle to 'Remove from Compare'; header Compare badge does not reach 1

Title: Add Second Product and Header Compare Count Reaches 2
Preconditions: One product already in the compare list (from CAT-COMP-001); compare is localStorage-backed (guest or authenticated)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a different product detail page
[WAIT] PDP fully loaded
[ACT] Click 'Add to Compare' on the second product
[WAIT] Wait for the success toast and header badge to update
[ACT] Click the header 'Compare' link to open /compare
Assertions:
[DOM] Second product's button toggles to 'Remove from Compare' (aria-pressed=true)
[STATE] Header 'Compare' link badge count reaches 2 (there is NO floating compare bar — the count lives in the header link)
[DOM] The /compare page lists both products side-by-side with their properties (and a 'Show differences only' checkbox + 'Clear list' button)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Header Compare badge does not reach 2; second product not added; /compare page does not show both products

Title: Navigate to Compare Page
Preconditions: User logged in; 2+ products in compare bar
Steps:
[ACT] Click 'Compare' button in the compare bar
[WAIT] Compare page loads
[ASSERT] Verify compare page displays products side-by-side
Assertions:
[NAV] URL contains /compare or similar comparison path
[DOM] Products displayed in columns with images and non-empty names
[DOM] Product properties/attributes shown in aligned rows for comparison
[DOM] Prices displayed for each product (each price > 0)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors on compare page load
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on page load
Failure_Signals: Compare page blank; products not displayed side-by-side; 404 on compare URL

Title: Compare Product Properties Side-by-Side
Preconditions: User on compare page with 2+ products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to compare page with 2+ products
[WAIT] Compare table/grid fully rendered
[SCROLL] Scroll through property rows
[ASSERT] Verify all shared properties are aligned across columns
Assertions:
[DOM] Product names displayed as non-empty column headers
[DOM] Product images visible for each product (non-empty src)
[DOM] Properties (brand, size, color, or similar attributes) aligned in rows
[DOM] Prices shown with correct formatting (each price matches /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/)
[FORMAT] All prices display with exactly 2 decimal places
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Properties misaligned between products; missing product images; prices not displayed; empty property rows

Title: Remove Product from Comparison
Preconditions: User on compare page with 2+ products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to compare page with 3 products
[WAIT] All 3 products displayed
[ACT] Click 'Remove' or 'X' on any one product
[WAIT] Product removed from comparison grid
Assertions:
[DOM] Removed product no longer shown in compare grid
[STATE] 2 remaining products still displayed correctly
[DOM] Properties re-aligned for remaining products
[DOM] Compare bar updated to show 2 items
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Removed product still visible; remaining products misaligned; page crashes on remove; compare bar count not updated

Title: Max Products Limit Enforcement
Preconditions: User logged in; catalog has sufficient products to reach the compare limit
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category listing on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Add products to compare via PDP or listing checkboxes until compare bar shows the maximum allowed count
[WAIT] Compare bar shows maximum count
[NAV] Navigate to any additional product PDP
[ACT] Click 'Compare' button on that additional product
[WAIT] Wait for response
Assertions:
[DOM] Error message or tooltip shown indicating max compare limit reached
[STATE] Additional product NOT added to compare
[DOM] Compare bar still shows the same maximum count (unchanged)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Additional product added without limit; no error message shown; compare bar count exceeds maximum

Title: Compare from Catalog Listing - Select Products
Preconditions: Category page with multiple products; compare is localStorage-backed (guest or authenticated)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Product grid loaded
[ACT] Click 'Add to Compare' on the first product card
[WAIT] Card button toggles to 'Remove from Compare'
[ACT] Click 'Add to Compare' on the second product card
[WAIT] Wait for the header Compare badge to update
Assertions:
[DOM] Each product card exposes an 'Add to Compare' button (not a checkbox) that toggles to 'Remove from Compare' with aria-pressed
[STATE] First product card shows 'Remove from Compare' (selected) state
[STATE] Second product card shows 'Remove from Compare' (selected) state
[DOM] Header 'Compare' nav link badge shows 2 (count lives in the header link; there is NO floating compare bar)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: 'Add to Compare' button missing on product cards; header Compare badge does not reach 2 after selecting 2 products

Title: Compare from Catalog Listing - Navigate to Compare Page
Preconditions: User on category page with 2 products selected for compare
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page
[ACT] Select 2 products for compare via checkboxes
[WAIT] Compare bar visible with 2 products
[ACT] Click 'Compare' button in compare bar
[WAIT] Compare page loads
Assertions:
[NAV] URL navigates to compare page
[DOM] Both selected products displayed on compare page with non-empty names
[DOM] Properties aligned for comparison
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: Compare page does not load; selected products not shown on compare page; 404 error

Title: Clear All from Compare
Preconditions: User has 2+ products in compare
Steps:
[ACT] With products in compare bar click 'Clear All' or 'Remove All'
[WAIT] Compare bar clears
[ASSERT] Verify no products in compare
Assertions:
[DOM] Compare bar disappears or shows empty state
[STATE] No products in compare list
[DOM] Compare indicators removed from all product cards/PDPs
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no GraphQL errors
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Compare bar still shows products after clear; compare indicators persist on cards; clear button missing
